African travel guide and booking directory for regions, countries, attractions, stays, dining, and trusted partner booking paths. Directory-first planning with partner booking links for Africa travel.

Image slot Restaurants Index Hero
  • Home
  • Restaurants

Restaurants

Restaurant listings help travelers understand the wider experience around each attraction.

Dining is tied back to place, not treated as an afterthought. Browse by country or region, then open the full landing page for more detail.

Image slot Restaurant Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp Dining

Uganda • Lodge dining

Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp Dining

A realistic dining option for Bwindi because most visitors eat at their lodge before and after trekking rather than browsing standalone restaurants.

★ 4.5 (126) $$$
Seasonal camp menus and packed trekking lunches Bwindi Impenetrable National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Paraa Safari Lodge Dining

Uganda • Lodge dining

Paraa Safari Lodge Dining

Murchison dining is usually lodge-based because game drives and boat departures shape the day. Paraa is practical for travelers staying near the river.

★ 4.3 (218) $$
Buffet meals and Nile-view lodge dining Murchison Falls National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Governors Camp Dining

Kenya • Safari camp dining

Governors' Camp Dining

The Mara is not a city restaurant destination; dining is usually tied to camp quality, guide timing, and whether bush meals are included.

★ 4.6 (311) $$$
Camp breakfasts, bush meals, and classic safari dinners Maasai Mara
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Kenya • Safari lodge dining

Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Ol Tukai dining works because visitors are usually inside the park for early and late game drives, making lodge meals more realistic than off-site restaurant runs.

★ 4.4 (205) $$
Lodge buffet meals with Amboseli wetland and mountain context Amboseli National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Serengeti Serena Dining

Tanzania • Safari lodge dining

Serengeti Serena Safari Lodge Dining

In the Serengeti, dining realism is about reliable camp meals and packed lunches because wildlife movement and distances dominate the day.

★ 4.4 (244) $$$
Lodge meals and packed safari lunches Serengeti National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant The Rock Restaurant Zanzibar

Tanzania • Zanzibari seafood

The Rock Restaurant Zanzibar

The Rock is a recognizable Zanzibar dining stop for travelers who want one memorable meal between Stone Town culture and beach time.

★ 4.3 (1,028) $$$
Seafood platters and ocean-view dining Zanzibar
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Seebamboes Cape Town

South Africa • Contemporary South African seafood

Seebamboes

Seebamboes reflects Cape Town s current food appeal: local produce, seafood, and a more contemporary expression of South African dining.

★ 4.6 (96) $$$
Cape seafood, local produce, and modern South African flavors Cape Town
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Kruger Shalati Dining

South Africa • Safari hotel dining

Kruger Shalati Dining

Dining at Kruger Shalati is part of the stay, with meals shaped around game drives and the Sabie River setting.

★ 4.5 (188) $$$$
Bridge hotel meals, bush breakfasts, and South African lodge dinners Kruger National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Lookout Cafe Victoria Falls

Zimbabwe • Casual Zambezi gorge dining

Lookout Cafe

Lookout Cafe is a realistic Victoria Falls stop because it pairs easily with town-based activities and views over the Batoka Gorge.

★ 4.5 (743) $$
Lunches, cocktails, and gorge views Victoria Falls
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Kabana Rooftop

Morocco • Modern Moroccan and international

Kabana Rooftop

Kabana works for Marrakech because it gives visitors a polished rooftop meal near the medina after dense souk and garden touring.

★ 4.4 (812) $$$
Tagines, mezze, seafood, and rooftop sunset drinks Marrakech & Atlas Gateway
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Sabyinyo Silverback Lodge Dining

Rwanda • Luxury lodge dining

Sabyinyo Silverback Lodge Dining

Volcanoes dining is normally lodge-based because gorilla trekking starts early and travelers return tired from steep forest trails.

★ 4.6 (143) $$$$
Multi-course lodge meals and trekking-day breakfasts Volcanoes National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Ben Abeba

Ethiopia • Ethiopian and international

Ben Abeba

Ben Abeba is one of Lalibela s best-known traveler restaurants and pairs well with church touring because it adds landscape and evening atmosphere.

★ 4.5 (389) $$
Injera platters, local stews, and sunset-view dining Lalibela
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Oasis Beach Resort Restaurant

Ghana • Ghanaian and seafood

Oasis Beach Resort Restaurant

Cape Coast dining is casual and practical. Oasis works as a traveler-friendly stop after castle touring or before a Kakum day.

★ 4.2 (274) $$
Grilled fish, jollof rice, and casual beachfront meals Cape Coast & Kakum
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Les Paletuviers Restaurant

Senegal • Senegalese and seafood lodge dining

Les Paletuviers Restaurant

In the delta, the best dining is often at the lodge because boat transfers and evening light shape the experience.

★ 4.3 (112) $$
Fresh fish, Senegalese sauces, and lodge meals Sine-Saloum Delta
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Casa Del Papa Restaurant

Benin • Beninese and coastal dining

Casa del Papa Restaurant

This is a practical meal stop for travelers using Casa del Papa as a comfortable Ouidah-area base rather than hunting for nightlife-style dining.

★ 4.2 (138) $$
Grilled fish, local sauces, and resort meals Ouidah & Ganvié
View Details
Image slot Restaurant The Place Resort Restaurant

Sierra Leone • Beach seafood

The Place Resort Restaurant

Beach dining on the peninsula is strongest when tied to the resort or beach base, especially for travelers who want predictable service and transfers.

★ 4.3 (167) $$
Grilled lobster, fish, rice dishes, and beach meals Tokeh & River No. 2 Beach
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Barracuda Restaurant Sal

Cabo Verde • Cabo Verdean seafood

Barracuda Restaurant

Santa Maria has a real independent dining scene, and Barracuda fits travelers who want seafood outside the resort buffet rhythm.

★ 4.3 (421) $$
Grilled tuna, lobster, cachupa, and beach-town seafood Sal Island
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Camp Okavango Dining

Botswana • Delta camp dining

Camp Okavango Dining

Okavango dining is normally included in the camp stay because there are no casual standalone restaurant circuits inside the Delta.

★ 4.5 (91) $$$$
Camp meals, high tea, and safari-day dining Okavango Delta
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Sossusvlei Lodge Restaurant

Namibia • Desert lodge dining

Sossusvlei Lodge Restaurant

This restaurant is practical because desert days start early and travelers need dependable meals close to the Sossusvlei access gate.

★ 4.3 (284) $$
Grill dinners, breakfast before dune excursions, and lodge buffets Namib Desert
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Mfuwe Lodge Dining

Zambia • Safari lodge dining

Mfuwe Lodge Dining

South Luangwa meals are usually attached to camps and lodges because activities start early and the park is not a casual restaurant destination.

★ 4.5 (133) $$$
Lodge meals and safari-day dining South Luangwa National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Desert Luxury Camp Dining

Morocco • Moroccan camp dining

Desert Luxury Camp Dining

In the dunes, the honest dining experience is camp-based rather than a standalone restaurant. The meal is part of the overnight Sahara rhythm.

★ 4.4 (176) $$$
Tagine, couscous, mint tea, and campfire dinners Sahara Dunes
View Details
Image slot Restaurant 9 Pyramids Lounge

Egypt • Egyptian and international

9 Pyramids Lounge

9 Pyramids Lounge is a practical Giza dining option because it keeps travelers close to the plateau rather than adding a cross-city transfer.

★ 4.3 (685) $$$
Mezze, grilled dishes, and pyramid-view dining Cairo & Giza
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Dar Zarrouk

Tunisia • Tunisian Mediterranean

Dar Zarrouk

Dar Zarrouk is a logical Sidi Bou Said meal for travelers pairing Tunis, Carthage, and the blue-and-white village in one cultural circuit.

★ 4.3 (477) $$$
Couscous, seafood, brik, and sea-view dining Tunis & Sidi Bou Said
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Terres Touareg Camp Dining

Algeria • Tuareg desert camp dining

Terres Touareg Camp Dining

For Tassili, dining is part of the guided expedition. A camp cook and simple meals are more realistic than a restaurant listing in the rock-art zones.

★ 4.4 (68) $$$
Camp meals, tea, couscous, and simple desert expedition food Djanet & Tassili n’Ajjer
View Details

Trek Africa Guide

African travel guide and booking directory for regions, countries, attractions, stays, dining, and trusted partner booking paths.

Travelers can compare the route logic, nearby stays, dining, and practical access notes on Trek Africa Guide before continuing to external booking partners.

Affiliate notice: booking buttons redirect to external partner pages for the selected listing. Always verify live availability, rates, and final booking terms on the partner side.
